metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Split AC2 into verifiable sub-criteria each with a concrete proving command: (a) `pytest tests/unit/test_code_severity_attrs.py -v` asserting CodeSeverityAttrs fields and defaults; (b) a test asserting the verifier emits exactly one LLM call; (c) `pytest tests/unit/test_det_enrichment.py -v` covering churn=0 fallback and path-based reversibility detection.
findings.finding: AC2 bundles four distinct deliverables (CodeSeverityAttrs schema, aggregate-call wiring, DET enrichment with git churn, fallback behavior) under effort-framed language ('implemented') with no proving command, making it neither fully measurable nor verifiable as done.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Proving commands: (a) `pytest tests/unit/test_code_severity_attrs.py -v` — asserts all new fields present with correct defaults; (b) `pytest tests/unit/test_verify_call_count.py -v` — asserts single aggregate LLM call; (c) `pytest tests/unit/test_det_enrichment.py -v` — asserts churn_90d computation and freq_mult=0.5 fallback when git unavailable.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify whether `impact_fn` was actually landed in `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` by the sibling plan (fishable-apivorous-redhead); if not, explicitly add 'add `impact_fn: Callable | None` param to `pass3_decide` and `pass3_over_findings`' and 'create `impact_plan` for plan-review dispatch' to this plan's scope and AC.
findings.finding: The plan's central enabling premise — '`impact_fn` param ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead; code-review now passes `impact_fn=impact_code`' — is false. Reading `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` lines 131-214 confirms `pass3_decide` and `pass3_over_findings` have no `impact_fn` parameter. A full-repo grep finds zero occurrences of `impact_fn` anywhere in `src/`. The plan describes this as a pre-existing hook that this plan merely uses; in reality, adding it is in-scope work that is not called out in the AC or scope.
findings.location: What section: 'dispatched per-gate off the shared decide.pass3_decide via the impact_fn param (that param ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'Add `impact_fn: Callable[[dict], float] | None = None` to `pass3_decide` and `pass3_over_findings` in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`; create `impact_plan` in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/` and wire it as the plan-review dispatch.' Update AC3 to prove-command both the plan-review unchanged path AND the new parameter's existence.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either confirm `impact_plan` was landed by the sibling plan (provide a grep-verifiable location) or add its creation to this plan's scope and AC3's proving command.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `impact_plan` 'is still dispatched' by plan-review (AC3: 'a test asserts plan-review's decide path is unchanged (impact_plan still dispatched; impact_code never touches it)'). A full-repo grep of `src/` finds zero occurrences of `impact_plan`. This is either new work that the sibling plan was supposed to deliver (but did not land) or new work this plan must create — either way, the descoping claim 'Plan-review is byte-unchanged' and the AC3 test premise are both unverifiable / false.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Plan-review impact unchanged'; AC3: 'impact_plan still dispatched; impact_code never touches it'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's assertion that `impact_fn` already exists on `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings`: it does not exist in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py`; explicitly designate adding it (with a `None` default to keep plan-review callers byte-unchanged) as a creation task and list it in Scope.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `impact_fn` on `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` 'ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead', but `impact_fn` is absent from the entire `src/` tree. The actual `pass3_decide` signature (decide.py:131–136) and `pass3_over_findings` (decide.py:195–214) have no such parameter. This is a hallucinated existing symbol — adding it is a creation/behavioral hunk, not a wire-up.
findings.location: What section: 'the `impact_fn` param (that param ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: '`src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` — add `impact_fn: Callable[[dict], float] | None = None` to both `pass3_decide` and `pass3_over_findings`, defaulting to the existing `impact()` when None so all plan-review callers remain byte-unchanged.' Remove the parenthetical claiming it already exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm `impact_fn` param exists in `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` (from fishable-apivorous-redhead) BEFORE implementing the dispatch; if the dependency is not yet merged, add an explicit ordering constraint or include the param addition in this plan's scope.
findings.finding: The plan's central dispatch mechanism (`code_review_decide` passing `impact_fn=impact_code` through `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings`) depends on an `impact_fn` parameter the plan asserts 'ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with fishable-apivorous-redhead,' but the live `decide.py` and `orchestrator.py` contain NO `impact_fn` parameter — `pass3_decide` calls `impact(attrs)` directly with no injection point. The dependency ticket is `in_progress`. If the implementer takes the plan at face value and skips adding the param (treating it as pre-existing), the dispatch mechanism won't compile.
findings.location: Implementation mechanics section: 'PER-GATE DISPATCH: code_review_decide passes impact_fn=impact_code'; AC line 3: 'a test asserts plan-review's decide path is unchanged (impact_plan still dispatched; impact_code never touches it)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Depends on fishable-apivorous-redhead (must be merged first)' precondition in the Scope section, or explicitly include 'add impact_fn: Callable[[dict], float] | None = None to pass3_decide, defaulting to the existing impact fn' in this plan's Scope if the dependency might slip.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the `likelihood` field name collision: either rename the code-review likelihood field (e.g., `occurrence_likelihood` or `trigger_likelihood`) on `CodeSeverityAttrs` so it doesn't shadow the base `SeverityAttrs.likelihood`, or explicitly document that `CodeSeverityAttrs` overrides the base field definition with the new enum values and that `impact_code` never calls the base `impact()` on the same attrs dict.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new code-review `likelihood` enum (`common=1.0 / sometimes=0.6 / rare=0.25`) emitted as a field on `code_review_verification_model`, but the base `SeverityAttrs` that `CodeSeverityAttrs` EXTENDS already declares `likelihood: str` with values `low|medium|high` (mapped by `_LIKE01` in decide.py). Because `CodeSeverityAttrs` extends rather than replaces `SeverityAttrs`, both the old LLM-emitted `likelihood` (low/medium/high) and the new code-review `likelihood` (common/sometimes/rare) must coexist on the same field name, which is mechanically incoherent: `impact_code` reads `common/sometimes/rare`, but Pydantic will validate against the inherited field definition (low|medium|high), and the base `impact()` function will also attempt to interpret the field with `_LIKE01`.
findings.location: What section: 'LIKELIHOOD (LLM-emitted enum): common=1.0 / sometimes=0.6 / rare=0.25'; Implementation mechanics: 'CodeSeverityAttrs EXTENDING the shared 5 attrs'
findings.suggested_fix: Rename the new field to `trigger_likelihood: str = Field(default='common', description='common|sometimes|rare')` on `CodeSeverityAttrs`, and update `impact_code` to read `attrs.get('trigger_likelihood', 'common')`. This avoids any Pydantic field override complexity and makes the two vocabularies unambiguously distinct.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (via `grep -r impact_fn src/`) that the `impact_fn` parameter on `pass3_decide()` and `pass3_over_findings()` is present in the codebase before this story begins, or add an explicit scope note and precondition: 'Depends on fishable-apivorous-redhead landing `impact_fn`; this story must not begin until that dep is merged.'
findings.finding: The plan asserts `impact_fn` on `pass3_decide()` / `pass3_over_findings()` 'ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead', but reading the live `decide.py` shows `pass3_decide()` has no `impact_fn` parameter at all — the assertion is unverified against the current codebase HEAD.
findings.location: What section — 'that param ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead; code-review now passes `impact_fn=impact_code`'; also AC3 — 'Per-gate dispatch via `impact_fn=impact_code`; a test asserts plan-review's decide path is unchanged'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope or a new Preconditions section: 'PRECONDITION: confirm `pass3_decide(…, impact_fn=…)` and `pass3_over_findings(…, impact_fn=…)` exist in `review_kernel/decide.py` before starting. If absent (parent not yet merged), treat adding the `impact_fn` seam as in-scope for this story and update AC3 to include a test that the seam is present.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge that `impact_fn` does NOT yet exist on `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` and add its addition (with backward-compatible default `impact_fn=None` => use current `impact()`) explicitly to the scope and AC.
findings.finding: `impact_fn` parameter on `pass3_decide` and `pass3_over_findings` is referenced as already-existing but is confirmed absent from the current codebase — the plan's central dispatch mechanism rests on a non-existent API.
findings.location: What section: 'code-review now passes `impact_fn=impact_code`'; AC: 'Per-gate dispatch via `impact_fn=impact_code`'; Implementation mechanics: 'code_review_decide passes impact_fn=impact_code'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to scope: 'Add `impact_fn: Callable[[dict], float] | None = None` to `pass3_decide` and `pass3_over_findings`; when `None`, use the existing `impact()` function (plan-review path unchanged by construction).' Then the AC's isolation test is meaningful.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether `CodeSeverityAttrs` renames the `likelihood` field (e.g., to `occurrence_likelihood`) or shadows it with an explicit `common/sometimes/rare` description, and confirm the base `SeverityAttrs` `low/medium/high` vocabulary is untouched for plan-review.
findings.finding: The new `likelihood` enum (`common / sometimes / rare`) introduced for `impact_code` collides with the existing `likelihood` enum (`low / medium / high`) already on `SeverityAttrs` and mapped in `_LIKE01` — the plan never resolves which vocabulary the `likelihood` field on `CodeSeverityAttrs` carries, or whether the field is renamed.
findings.location: What section: 'LIKELIHOOD (LLM-emitted enum): common=1.0 / sometimes=0.6 / rare=0.25'; Implementation mechanics: 'CodeSeverityAttrs EXTENDING the shared 5 attrs'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to scope: register `code_review_verification` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/contracts.py` AND update `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` `output_schema: verification` → `output_schema: code_review_verification`.
findings.finding: `code_review_verification_model()` and `CodeSeverityAttrs` are named as a new registered contract (`code_review_verification`) but neither the registration site, the function definition location, nor the gate-yaml `output_schema` update is specified — leaving the schema-to-contract wiring incomplete.
findings.location: Implementation mechanics: 'Registered as a `code_review_verification` contract; code-review's verify step points at it'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether adding `impact_fn` to `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` is in scope for THIS plan (child fishable-apivorous-redhead did not land it); if yes, add an explicit AC and confirm the 'plan-review path byte-unchanged' assertion is tested end-to-end, not just by fixture. [T3 — Contradicted capability]
findings.finding: The plan asserts `impact_fn` on `pass3_decide`/`pass3_over_findings` 'ALREADY EXISTS — it landed with the plan-review impact redesign child fishable-apivorous-redhead', but reading `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/decide.py` directly shows neither function has an `impact_fn` parameter. This is a Contradicted capability: the entire per-gate dispatch mechanism (`code_review_decide` passing `impact_fn=impact_code`) depends on infrastructure that does not exist.
findings.location: What section: 'A code-review-specific, verifier-embedded impact model (`decide.impact_code`) dispatched per-gate off the shared `decide.pass3_decide` via the `impact_fn` param (that param ALREADY EXISTS…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope and ACs: 'Add `impact_fn: Callable[[dict], float] | None = None` to `pass3_decide` and `pass3_over_findings`; default `None` falls back to the existing `impact(attrs)` function so plan-review is byte-unchanged. A test asserts `pass3_decide(verif)` (no `impact_fn`) produces identical output before and after the change.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an acceptance criterion (or extend AC2) that verifies the code-review verify prompt explicitly elicits all ten tier-tagged binaries, likelihood, silent_failure, and escapes_automation — e.g., a prompt-content unit test or a contract test asserting the LLM is asked for each new field by name.
findings.finding: The verify prompt update is listed as a named scope deliverable ('the verify prompt' under src/rebar/llm/code_review/) — it must emit all new fields (the ten tier-tagged binaries, likelihood enum, silent_failure, escapes_automation) — but no acceptance criterion explicitly covers or validates the prompt content or its emitted fields.
findings.location: Scope section: 'src/rebar/llm/code_review/ (verify-step contract wiring, the DET enrichment in the decide op, the verify prompt)'; AC2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit test cases in `tests/unit/test_impact_code.py` (and require them in AC 1/AC 2) for: (a) git unavailable / no file attributable => `freq_mult == 0.5`; (b) `likelihood` key absent from attrs => multiplier treated as `common` (1.0); (c) `silent_failure` / `escapes_automation` absent => `amp == 0.8`.
findings.finding: The git-unavailable fallback path (churn_90d=0 => freq_mult=0.5) and the absent-metadata defaults (likelihood absent => 'common' i.e. 1.0; detection fields absent => amp=0.8) are new behaviors explicitly called out as correctness guarantees in the design, but no AC requires explicit tests for these edge/failure cases — they are only implied by the vague phrase 'unit-tested for each multiplier + the gate'.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 1 ('Unit-tested for each multiplier + the gate') and AC 2 ('churn=0/freq=0.5 fallback when git is unavailable')
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC 1 to read: 'Unit-tested for each multiplier + the gate, **including fallback/default paths**: git unavailable => freq_mult=0.5; likelihood absent => mult=1.0 (common); detection fields absent => amp=0.8.' Add one test function per fallback (three assertions in test_impact_code.py) using a stub/mock for the git subprocess in the unavailable case.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the schema definition where `silent_failure` and `escapes_automation` live (e.g., as fields on `CodeSeverityAttrs` so `impact_code(attrs)` reads `attrs.get('silent_failure', False)`) and confirm `impact_code`'s input dict covers both lane binaries and detection booleans.
findings.finding: The placement of `silent_failure` and `escapes_automation` (detection-amplifier booleans) in the output schema is unspecified — the plan says they are 'LLM-emitted booleans in `code_review_verification_model`' but does not say whether they appear in `binary`, in `severity_attributes`/`CodeSeverityAttrs`, or as top-level fields on the verification object, leaving `impact_code`'s read path ambiguous.
findings.location: What section: 'DETECTION AMPLIFIER: amp = 1.0 if (silent_failure or escapes_automation) else 0.8. Both are LLM-emitted booleans in `code_review_verification_model`'; Implementation mechanics: 'emit ... silent_failure / escapes_automation (bool) as NEW fields on a code_review_verification_model()'
findings.suggested_fix: Add them to `CodeSeverityAttrs` (boolean fields, default `False`) so `impact_code(attrs)` can read them from the same dict that carries the tier-tagged binary results — consistent with how `impact(attrs)` currently reads `prod_impact` etc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (or correct) the fixture path: `tests/unit/fixtures/` does not exist — either confirm the intent is to CREATE this directory, or use the existing `tests/fixtures/` root instead, and update the AC4 proving command path to match.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` cannot exist at that path because neither `tests/unit/fixtures/` nor any `fixtures/` subdirectory under `tests/unit/` currently exists. The only `tests/fixtures/` directory is at the top-level `tests/fixtures/` (containing `bridge_convergence/`, `jira/`, `review_bot_merge/`, `ticket-cli-baseline.json`). The plan's stated path is a hallucinated directory.
findings.location: AC4: '`tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` exists'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the fixture path in AC4 to `tests/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` to match the existing top-level fixtures directory, OR explicitly state that `tests/unit/fixtures/` will be created as a new directory.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out path for the scoring model change (e.g., an `impact_fn` feature flag that can revert to the old function, or a shadow-scoring period where both models run and are logged before the new one goes live).
findings.finding: Dropping the 0.33 attribute floor and introducing a new two-lane scoring model is a behavioral breaking change to all in-flight and future code-review pipeline outputs, but the plan specifies no rollback path, feature-flag, or expand-contract sequence. If the new model mis-scores findings in production before the calibration child completes, there is no back-out mechanism.
findings.location: What section — 'RE-ANCHOR: drop the 0.33 attribute floor'; Implementation mechanics — calibration child deferred to raptorial-galloping-dragon
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-paragraph 'Rollback' section: keep the old impact function importable (e.g., as `impact_code_v0`), wire a config/env flag to `code_review_decide` so `impact_fn` can be switched back to `impact_code_v0` without a code deploy, and add an AC that asserts this flag works.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named proving command for AC4, e.g. `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_impact_calibration.py -v`, and clarify whether this test lives in `test_impact_code.py` or a separate file.
findings.finding: AC4 describes a calibration test with a concrete numeric margin but provides no proving command, leaving the separation criterion unverifiable from CI.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC4: 'Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_impact_calibration.py -v` (or add a `test_calibration_separation` test class to `test_impact_code.py` and note it explicitly).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in Scope that `tests/unit/fixtures/` must be created and that the initial 31-row `code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` is a greenfield authoring task; add it as a concrete deliverable line in AC4.
findings.finding: The plan references `tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` (AC4) and `tests/unit/test_impact_code.py` (AC1 proving command) as files to be created. These do not exist, which is expected for new deliverables — but the `tests/unit/fixtures/` directory itself does not exist, meaning the plan implicitly requires directory creation that is not called out in scope or AC.
findings.location: AC4: 'tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl exists'; AC1: proving command 'pytest tests/unit/test_impact_code.py -v'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact `git log` flag combination for `churn_90d` at the flag level (e.g. `git log --follow --name-only --format='' --after='90 days ago' -- <file> | grep -c .`) so implementers cannot silently use a variant that miscounts. [T3 — Unverified subcommand surface]
findings.finding: The `git log` subcommand for computing `churn_90d` is never specified at the flag level — the plan states the semantic goal ('commits touching the finding's file in the last 90 days') and the fallback, but names no specific flags (e.g. `--after`, `--since`, `--follow`, `--name-only`, `--format=''`). Flag-level mismatches in git log (e.g. `--since=90.days` vs `--after=90 days ago` vs an explicit date) produce silently wrong counts.
findings.location: What section / Implementation mechanics: 'churn_90d = commits touching the finding's file in the last 90 days (`git log`, DET)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Implementation mechanics section, add: 'Proven command: `git -C <repo_root> log --follow --name-only --format='' --after="90 days ago" -- <file_path> | grep -c .`; wrap in a try/except so any non-zero exit or OSError falls back to churn_90d=0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the reversibility surface-detection rule as a closed set of path patterns or a callable (e.g., glob list or function in `detectors.py`) that `code_review_decide`'s DET enrichment can reference deterministically.
findings.finding: The reversibility-floor surface-detection rule references prose categories ('migration dir, exported public API, persisted/serialization format, deletion, released artifact') without defining a closed enumeration or path-pattern list, making the DET detection logic underspecified for an implementer.
findings.location: What section: 'REVERSIBILITY FLOOR (det/hybrid), GATED: if the change touches a hard-to-reverse surface (migration dir, exported public API, persisted/serialization format, deletion, released artifact)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly call out in the plan (Scope or Implementation mechanics) that the old impact baseline function (or its pre-computed scores on the labeled fixture) must be preserved/archived so AC4's comparison test can reference it.
findings.finding: AC4 requires the calibration test to assert that `median(impact_code[HIGH]) - median(impact_code[NIT])` is strictly greater than the old mean impact's gap on the same fixture, but the plan does not describe how the old impact baseline function is preserved or made available for comparison after the model is replaced.
findings.location: AC4: 'strictly GREATER than the old mean impact's gap on the same fixture'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to Implementation mechanics: 'Archive the current mean-based impact scores on code_review_impact_labels.jsonl as a frozen baseline column in the fixture (or as a snapshot dict in the test module) before replacing the function, so AC4 can compare without importing deleted code.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Designate a single constants/config home (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/impact_constants.py` or a config section) for all tunable numerics (tier values, likelihood multipliers, freq_mult parameters, reversibility_floor, amp values) so the calibration child can update them in one place without grep-and-edit across the implementation.
findings.finding: The tier values (0.3/0.6/0.9), likelihood multipliers (1.0/0.6/0.25), freq_mult formula parameters (saturation at 30, floor 0.5), reversibility_floor (0.6), and amp values (0.8/1.0) are all specified as hardcoded constants, yet the plan explicitly acknowledges a calibration child will tune thresholds and 'co-calibrate tier values + block threshold on the labeled set' — meaning these values are expected to evolve. No mention is made of housing them in a config, constants module, or any structure that makes future tuning a single-file change rather than a grep-and-edit across the implementation.
findings.location: Implementation mechanics / AC line 1 (impact_code formula) / 'Re-anchor' bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'constants home' bullet to the Scope section, e.g.: 'All numeric parameters (tier weights, likelihood multipliers, freq_mult formula, amp, reversibility_floor) live in `src/rebar/llm/review_kernel/impact_constants.py`; `impact_code` imports them; the calibration child modifies only that file.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope any developer-facing doc that enumerates verify contracts (CLAUDE.md, a contracts README, or pipeline overview) to be updated alongside the new `code_review_verification` contract registration, or confirm in the plan that no such doc exists and none is needed.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `code_review_verification` contract registered in the contract registry (distinct from the existing shared `verification` contract), but does not call out updating any developer-facing documentation that describes the contract system (e.g., CLAUDE.md, a contracts README, or a pipeline overview doc). If such docs enumerate known contracts or describe how verify steps are wired, they will become stale after this registration.
findings.location: Implementation mechanics — 'Registered as a `code_review_verification` contract; code-review's verify step points at it'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that `churn_90d` is computed once per unique file path (not once per finding) and cached/reused across findings that share a file, to avoid redundant git subprocess calls.
findings.finding: The plan computes churn_90d via `git log` 'per finding' with no mention of deduplication or caching by file path, meaning N findings on the same file trigger N identical git log calls.
findings.location: Implementation mechanics — 'code_review_decide computes churn_90d (git) + the reversibility surface (changed-file paths) per finding'
findings.suggested_fix: Collect all finding file paths, deduplicate, run one `git log --follow` per unique file before the finding loop, store results in a dict keyed by file path, then look up by path when enriching each finding.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or implementation note requiring that a log warning (at minimum) is emitted whenever the git churn fallback is triggered, so operators can detect systematic git-unavailability affecting impact scores.
findings.finding: The plan provides no observability (logging, metric, or trace) for the git-call failure path; errors are silently swallowed into the churn=0/freq_mult=0.5 fallback, leaving operators unable to detect systematic git failures that cause all impact scores to silently use the floor multiplier.
findings.location: Implementation mechanics — 'FALLBACK: git unavailable / shallow clone / no file attributable => churn_90d = 0 => freq_mult = 0.5 (the minimum). Best-effort; never fails the review.'
findings.suggested_fix: Emit a `logger.warning('churn_90d fallback: git unavailable for %s, reason: %s', file_path, exc)` in the except/fallback branch; optionally increment a metric counter `code_review.git_churn_fallback_total`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` as a consumer that imports `decide.impact` directly, and confirm whether 're-anchor: drop the 0.33 attribute floor' modifies the existing `impact()` (affecting eval_scorers) or only the new `impact_code()` (leaving eval_scorers untouched).
findings.finding: `src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py` (outside the plan's Scope) directly imports and calls `rebar.llm.review_kernel.decide.impact` (line 132) to compute the impact score for eval cases. This is an unenumerated consumer of the `decide.impact()` function. If the plan's 're-anchor: drop the 0.33 attribute floor' modifies the existing `impact()` function rather than leaving it untouched, `eval_scorers.py` will silently change behavior without being listed as an edit target.
findings.location: Scope section (does not list eval_scorers.py); 'RE-ANCHOR: drop the 0.33 attribute floor'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`, and `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` as callers of `pass3_over_findings` that will be unaffected only if `impact_fn` defaults to `None` (falling back to the existing `impact()`), and add an explicit statement of that default contract to the Scope section.
findings.finding: Three callers of `pass3_over_findings` outside `src/rebar/llm/code_review/` are not enumerated in the plan's Scope: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` (line 661), `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py` (line 381), and `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` (line 363). If adding `impact_fn` to the kernel `pass3_over_findings` signature uses a non-None default that changes the fallback behavior, or if the signature change itself requires a positional update, all three callers are silently affected.
findings.location: Scope section; 'PER-GATE DISPATCH: code_review_decide passes impact_fn=impact_code. Plan-review is byte-unchanged'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'The `impact_fn` param on `pass3_over_findings` defaults to `None`; when `None`, the kernel falls back to the existing `impact()` function, keeping `plan_review/orchestrator.py`, `plan_review/workflow_ops.py`, and `workflow/gate_dispatch.py` byte-unchanged.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale in the What section for the two-lane topology: explain why prod and maint lanes must be separately multiplied (e.g., 'prod correctness is never discounted by file churn; maint debt is — cross-lane MAX after multipliers, not before, preserves this distinction').
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for the two-lane topology specifically (prod_lane vs maint_lane as separate MAX computations with different multipliers) vs. a simpler single-lane approach with the same tier-tagged binaries and a single frequency multiplier applied uniformly. The 'Why' section explains the general problem (mean dilutes maint findings) but doesn't affirmatively argue why the additional complexity of two independently-multiplied lanes — rather than one tiered lane with binary-level lane tags used only for routing the frequency multiplier — is the better design.
findings.location: Why section; What section: 'TWO LANES: prod_lane = MAX(correctness tier-tagged binaries) * likelihood_mult; maint_lane = MAX(maintainability tier-tagged binaries) * freq_mult'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the TWO LANES bullet: 'Two separate lanes preserve the invariant that a prod-correctness finding is never dampened by churn (likelihood_mult on prod, freq_mult on maint only); a single-lane MAX-after-multiplier would conflate these discount policies.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief prior-art comparison to the plan (or the promised ADR) explaining why the two-lane tier-tagged MAX model is preferred over the two most obvious alternatives — a weighted mean of the same binaries and a CVSS-style multiplicative (exploitability × impact) composition — so the novelty is justified, not merely asserted.
findings.finding: The two-lane tier-tagged MAX impact model is a novel architectural pattern but the plan does not cite or dismiss the most directly comparable established approaches (CVSS BaseScore's exploitability×impact multiplicative composition, or a weighted-mean variant) before committing to it.
findings.location: What section — 'TWO LANES: prod_lane = MAX(...) * likelihood_mult; maint_lane = MAX(...) * freq_mult'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section (or in a dedicated 'Alternatives considered' subsection that will be mirrored in the ADR), add one paragraph: 'Weighted mean: dilutes a single serious binary with minor ones — the same flaw as the existing mean, just with different weights. CVSS-multiplicative: couples two independent lanes, inflating prod findings when maint is low and vice versa. Two-lane MAX preserves independence and avoids dilution; the tier values + labeled calibration set are the empirical guard against over-inflation.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each serious-tier binary (`data_loss_without_recovery`, `security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere`, `silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision`, `unversioned_published_contract_break`, `safety_net_removal_without_replacement`), state the charitable carve-out text or a template pattern in the plan (e.g., 'score false if the same guarantee is enforced elsewhere in the call chain').
findings.finding: The content of the 'charitable carve-outs' for each serious binary is mentioned but never specified, deferring a real prompt-engineering design decision to the executor.
findings.location: What section: 'Each serious binary carries its charitable carve-out in its verify-prompt definition'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
